title: If you buy a new car, you begin to notice other same cars (perception trigger)
html:
TL;DR

  • March 2021 micro-axiom: "If you buy a new car, you begin to notice other same cars. If you're bald, you begin to see other bald people more. (Applies with opportunities/business). If you're around certain ideas and concepts, you begin to see them more and how they cross/apply throughout."
  • Yahir's own articulation of the heightened perception trigger — what cognitive science calls the Baader-Meinhof / frequency-illusion effect — but applied as a deliberate-exposure strategy for opportunities and business.
  • Implicit thesis: choose what you want to see more of, then voluntarily put yourself near the seed instances. The mechanism that makes mentors-from-afar, crypto club, and FBLA orbit-seeking actually work.
  • Genre: classic manifesto style note — three lines, no todos, no context, just a frame.
  • Late-March 2021 timing places this between the Ford interview prep and the crypto internship note — Yahir is actively seeding his own perception across both networks.

Source quote

"If you buy a new car, you begin to notice other same cars
If you're bald, you begin to see other bald people more
(Applies with opportunities/ business)
If you're around certain ideas and concepts, you begin to see them more and how they cross/ apply throughout"

Open questions

  • Did Yahir formalize this into an action — i.e., deliberately place himself near specific concept-clusters in spring 2021? The crypto-club / Ford-orbit moves suggest yes.
  • Is this the earliest perception-trigger articulation in the vault, or does an earlier note pre-date it?
  • Pairs naturally with surface area of luck — does Yahir later combine the two frames explicitly?
